case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for acetaldehyde on the provided surface.
case_metadata.notes: Best-available reconstruction from sanitized filename CC_O and formula C2H4O

plan.reasoning: History is empty so this is the first proposal. SMILES CC=O matches acetaldehyde with carbonyl O (index 2, SP2, lone pairs) as the natural donor; no radicals present so adsorbate_type=Molecule. Start with the most chemically plausible high-symmetry site: O-bound ontop Zr. This satisfies all site-alignment and chemical-type rules.

attempt_records.history_entry: 【✅ Perfect Adsorption】 ontop @ ['Al'] (Index [2]) -> Site: ontop (Al) | E=-0.650 eV

plan.reasoning: History shows only ontop Zr (O index 2, E=-0.820 eV, current best) and ontop Al (E=-0.650 eV) tested. Both succeeded without dissociation. Bridge Al-Zr remains untested per available sites list and is a chemically plausible next high-symmetry site for eta1-O binding. No convergence to known best or unstable slips observed, so continue. Adsorbate is stable Molecule; O (index 2) has lone pairs and is the correct donor.
